homepage.heroSubline: Horizon Pest Control keeps your home pest-free with local expertise. Serving Tampa Bay homes since 2011 with reliable, thorough service.
aboutText: Founded in 2011 by Tampa native Jason Miller, Horizon Pest Control started with one truck and a mission to protect local families from unwanted pests. What began as a small operation serving South Tampa bungalows has grown into a trusted name across Hillsborough County.

We take a science-based approach to pest management, using the latest techniques adapted for Florida's unique climate. Our team spends time understanding each home's specific needs, whether it's a historic bungalow in Seminole Heights or a new condo along Bayshore Boulevard.

As active members of the Tampa community, we're proud to support local schools and hurricane preparedness initiatives. When you choose Horizon, you're choosing neighbors who care about your family's safety and comfort.

valueProps.title: Local Climate Experts
valueProps.description: We understand how Tampa's humidity and seasonal rains create ideal conditions for pests, and tailor our approach accordingly.

valueProps.title: Historic Home Specialists
valueProps.description: Our technicians are trained in treating older Tampa homes without damaging their unique architectural features.

valueProps.title: Preventative Focus
valueProps.description: We don't just eliminate current pests - we help prevent future infestations through structural recommendations.

valueProps.title: Fast Emergency Response
valueProps.description: Available 24/7 for urgent situations like wasp nests or rodent sightings during hurricane season.

homepage.ctaSubline: Call our Tampa team today for a free inspection and customized protection plan.

services.name: Termite Inspections
services.shortDescription: Thorough termite evaluations for Tampa's high-risk homes.
services.description:
Tampa's combination of humidity, older housing stock, and frequent rain makes termite inspections crucial. Our certified inspectors examine every accessible area of your home, paying special attention to Florida's most vulnerable spots: the damp soil around foundations, wood siding on historic bungalows, and attic spaces in ranch-style homes.

We use moisture meters and infrared technology to detect hidden activity without unnecessary damage to your property. After the inspection, you'll receive a detailed report with photos and our recommendations, whether that's immediate treatment or preventative measures to protect your investment.

For homes in flood-prone areas like South Tampa or near the bay, we include additional checks for signs of Formosan termites, which thrive in our coastal climate.
process: Step 1: Exterior inspection of foundation, siding, and landscaping
Step 2: Interior examination of baseboards, windows, and vulnerable structural points
Step 3: Attic and crawl space evaluation with specialized tools
Step 4: Detailed report and consultation about findings
benefits: Early detection prevents costly structural damage
Peace of mind during swarming season (April-June)
Recognized by most Tampa-area homeowners' insurance providers
No-obligation assessment with transparent findings

faq.question: How often should Tampa homes be inspected for termites?
faq.answer: We recommend annual inspections for most homes, but older properties or those with previous termite activity may need biannual checks, especially after our rainy season from June-September.

faq.question: Do you inspect for drywood termites common in Florida?
faq.answer: Yes, our technicians are trained to identify both subterranean and drywood termite species, including the particularly destructive Formosan termites found along Tampa's coastline.

faq.question: Will the inspection disrupt my home?
faq.answer: Our non-invasive methods mean minimal disruption - we won't tear into walls or damage finishes. Most inspections take 45-90 minutes depending on your home's size.

services.name: Rodent Control
services.shortDescription: Humane yet effective solutions for Tampa's persistent rodent problems.
services.description:
From historic Ybor City buildings to newer Wesley Chapel developments, rodents find ways into Tampa Bay homes year-round. Our approach combines exclusion techniques with targeted treatments that are safe for families and pets.

We start by identifying how rodents are entering - often through damaged screens in Florida rooms, gaps in soffits on ranch homes, or cracks in older foundations. Our team then implements a three-phase plan: sealing entry points with rodent-proof materials, strategically placing tamper-resistant bait stations (out of reach of children and pets), and monitoring activity until the infestation is fully resolved.

For homes near Tampa's waterways or in agricultural areas like Brandon, we pay special attention to roof rats, which are particularly active during our mild winters when other food sources are scarce.
process: Step 1: Comprehensive inspection to locate entry points and nesting areas
Step 2: Customized exclusion work using durable materials
Step 3: Strategic placement of monitoring and bait stations
Step 4: Follow-up visits to ensure complete resolution
benefits: Reduces health risks from droppings and contamination
Prevents electrical wire damage common in Tampa attics
Humane methods that don't leave decaying rodents in walls
Long-term solutions rather than temporary fixes

faq.question: How quickly can you respond to a rodent sighting?
faq.answer: We prioritize rodent calls and typically can be onsite within 24-48 hours for urgent situations, faster than many Tampa providers during peak season (October-February).

faq.question: Do you handle roof rats common in Tampa palms?
faq.answer: Absolutely. Our technicians are experts at identifying and eliminating roof rat colonies that nest in palm trees and Spanish tile roofs throughout the area.

faq.question: Will treatment make my house smell bad?
faq.answer: We use methods that minimize odor. Any initial scent from bait stations dissipates quickly and is far less noticeable than the alternative of an active infestation.

services.name: Mosquito Control
services.shortDescription: Effective mosquito reduction for Tampa's backyard living spaces.
services.description:
Tampa's combination of humidity, standing water, and year-round warmth creates ideal mosquito breeding conditions. Our mosquito control program is designed specifically for Florida lifestyles where outdoor living is essential.

We treat not just your yard but the entire property perimeter, focusing on mosquito resting areas like shrubbery, under decks, and around pool cages. Our EPA-approved misting system reduces adult populations while larvicide treatments in standing water (common after our daily summer thunderstorms) prevent new generations from hatching.

For waterfront properties along Tampa Bay or homes near retention ponds in communities like Westchase, we recommend our premium service that includes monthly treatments from April through November - covering the entire mosquito season.

What sets us apart is our knowledge of local mosquito behavior. We know which species are active at different times of day in various Tampa neighborhoods, allowing us to time treatments for maximum effectiveness.
process: Step 1: Property assessment to identify breeding sites and resting zones
Step 2: Targeted barrier spray application to vegetation and structures
Step 3: Larvicide treatment for standing water areas
Step 4: Ongoing monitoring and adjustment based on rainfall and activity
benefits: Reduces mosquito populations by up to 90%
Safe for children, pets, and beneficial pollinators when properly applied
Helps protect against mosquito-borne illnesses prevalent in Florida
Extends usable outdoor living space in your yard or lanai

faq.question: How often do Tampa homes need mosquito treatments?
faq.answer: During peak season (May-October), we recommend every 3-4 weeks. In cooler months, every 6-8 weeks is usually sufficient unless you're near significant water sources.

faq.question: Will rain wash away the treatment?
faq.answer: Our products bond to foliage and become rainfast within hours. Heavy downpours may slightly reduce effectiveness, which is why we build weather patterns into our treatment schedules.

faq.question: Do you treat for no-see-ums too?
faq.answer: Yes, our barrier treatments reduce biting midges (no-see-ums) that plague Tampa beach areas and coastal neighborhoods, though complete elimination isn't possible for these persistent pests.

services.name: Bed Bug Treatments
services.shortDescription: Discreet, thorough bed bug elimination for Tampa homes and rentals.
services.description:
Tampa's tourism industry and transient population make bed bugs an ongoing concern in apartments, vacation rentals, and even single-family homes. Our comprehensive treatment combines multiple methods for complete eradication.

We start with a meticulous inspection using trained dogs and flashlights to locate all infestations - not just in beds but in furniture, baseboards, and electrical outlets where bed bugs hide. Treatment options include targeted heat application (particularly effective in Tampa's newer construction with drywall), steam treatments for delicate antiques in historic homes, and residual products when appropriate.

What sets us apart is our understanding of Tampa-specific challenges: we know bed bugs often travel in luggage through our international airport, hide in vacation rental linens near Busch Gardens, and spread through multi-unit buildings in downtown high-rises. Our protocols address these local patterns.
process: Step 1: Detailed inspection with canine teams and visual examination
Step 2: Custom treatment plan based on infestation severity and home type
Step 3: Application of chosen methods with focus on harborage areas
Step 4: Follow-up verification to confirm elimination
benefits: Higher success rates than DIY methods
Discreet service for sensitive situations
Minimal disruption to your daily routine
Prevention advice tailored to Tampa lifestyles

faq.question: How long does bed bug treatment take in Tampa homes?
faq.answer: Most single-family homes require 3-6 hours for initial treatment, with follow-up visits typically needed 2-3 weeks later. Condos or severe infestations may need multiple sessions.

faq.question: Will I need to dispose of my furniture?
faq.answer: Rarely. Our heat and steam treatments can save most items. We'll advise you honestly if any pieces are beyond treatment, which is uncommon with early detection.

faq.question: Do you treat adjacent units in condos or apartments?
faq.answer: We coordinate with property managers when needed and can treat surrounding units to prevent spread, following Tampa's multi-housing pest control protocols.

services.name: General Pest Control
services.shortDescription: Ongoing protection against Tampa's most common invaders.
services.description:
Our general pest control service forms the foundation of protection for Tampa homes against the area's most persistent pests: palmetto bugs, ants, spiders, and silverfish. We've tailored our approach to Florida's unique conditions where pests are active year-round.

Treatments vary by season - focusing on ant invasions during dry springs, palmetto bug prevention before summer rains, and spider control as temperatures cool in fall. Each visit includes interior and exterior treatment with products selected for your specific home type, whether it's a 1920s bungalow with original woodwork or a new construction home with spray foam insulation.

We pay special attention to Tampa's common problem areas: French drains that attract bugs, pool enclosures that harbor spiders, and lush landscaping that creates pest highways into homes. Your technician will point out these risk factors and suggest modifications to make your property less inviting to pests between treatments.
process: Step 1: Perimeter inspection and identification of pest activity
Step 2: Exterior barrier treatment with attention to entry points
Step 3: Interior spot treatment in problem areas as needed
Step 4: Detailed service report with findings and recommendations
benefits: Regular visits prevent small problems from becoming infestations
Flexible scheduling to fit your lifestyle
Reduces need for emergency treatments
Covers 30+ common Tampa area pests

faq.question: How often should Tampa homes receive pest control?
faq.answer: We recommend monthly or bimonthly service depending on your home's age, landscaping, and pest history. Older homes near water often benefit from more frequent visits.

faq.question: Is the treatment safe for my pets?
faq.answer: All products we use are EPA-approved for residential use when applied correctly. We'll provide specific instructions about keeping pets away until treatments dry (usually 1-2 hours).

faq.question: Do you treat for those huge palmetto bugs?
faq.answer: Yes, our exterior treatments specifically target American cockroaches (palmetto bugs) which are especially problematic in Tampa's older neighborhoods with mature oak trees and mulch beds.

reviews.name: Maria C.
reviews.text: Horizon found termite damage in our South Tampa bungalow that two other inspectors missed. Their detailed report with photos helped us get insurance coverage for the repairs. The technician explained everything clearly and even pointed out vulnerable spots in our historic home's foundation.

reviews.name: James T.
reviews.text: After years of battling mosquitoes in our backyard near the Hillsborough River, Horizon's monthly treatments finally let us enjoy our patio again. They noticed our irrigation system was creating standing water we hadn't even seen. Night and day difference - worth every penny.

reviews.name: Lisa & Dan R.
reviews.text: We had roof rats getting into our Seminole Heights attic through the palm trees. Horizon sealed all entry points humanely and installed protective guards on our trees. No more scratching noises at night and they followed up twice to make sure the problem was fully resolved.

reviews.name: Robert K.
reviews.text: As a Tampa Airbnb host, bed bugs were my worst nightmare. Horizon's heat treatment eliminated them completely after guests brought them in. They were discreet, thorough, and helped me implement prevention protocols. Six months later - still bed bug free.

reviews.name: Sarah J.
reviews.text: We've used Horizon's quarterly service for our Westchase home for three years. I appreciate how they adjust treatments seasonally - extra attention to ants in spring, palmetto bugs before summer rains. Our technician remembers our house's specific trouble spots each visit.

reviews.name: Carlos M.
reviews.text: Found termites in our Brandon home's garage during a renovation. Horizon's localized treatment saved us from tenting the whole house. They took time to explain the different termite species in our area and what signs to watch for in the future.

service-areas.title: Tampa Bay Pest Control Service Areas
service-areas.description: Horizon Pest Control serves Tampa, St. Pete, Clearwater, Brandon & Wesley Chapel with localized pest solutions for each community's needs.

serviceAreas.content: Horizon Pest Control provides reliable pest control services to the charming neighborhoods of St. Petersburg, FL. With its historic Florida cottage homes built between the 1920s and 1950s, the city’s unique architecture often requires specialized pest management solutions to protect against termites, ants, and other common pests. Our team understands the challenges posed by the hot, humid summers and tailors treatments to keep your home pest-free year-round. Whether you’re dealing with roaches, rodents, or mosquitoes, we offer eco-friendly and effective solutions to safeguard your property.
serviceAreas.localContext: St. Petersburg's historic homes and humid climate make it particularly susceptible to pests like termites and roaches.

serviceAreas.content: Serving Clearwater, FL, Horizon Pest Control specializes in protecting the city’s 1960s-70s ranch-style homes from common pests. The hot, humid summers create ideal conditions for ants, spiders, and palmetto bugs to thrive. Our targeted treatments are designed to address the specific needs of Clearwater’s residential properties, ensuring long-term pest prevention. From seasonal mosquito control to year-round termite inspections, we help homeowners maintain a comfortable and pest-free environment.
serviceAreas.localContext: Clearwater's ranch-style homes and coastal location make it prone to pests like spiders and palmetto bugs.

serviceAreas.content: Horizon Pest Control is your trusted pest management provider in Brandon, FL, where suburban developments from the 1980s-90s are common. The hot, humid climate attracts pests like termites, roaches, and rodents, which can quickly become a nuisance. Our team offers comprehensive pest control solutions tailored to Brandon’s residential neighborhoods, including preventative treatments and emergency services. Whether you need routine maintenance or a one-time extermination, we’ve got you covered.
serviceAreas.localContext: Brandon’s suburban homes and humid weather create favorable conditions for pests like termites and rodents.

serviceAreas.content: Horizon Pest Control serves the modern master-planned communities of Wesley Chapel, FL, with customized pest control solutions. The newer construction of homes (2000s-2020s) doesn’t make them immune to pests like ants, mosquitoes, and rodents, especially in the hot, humid summers. Our proactive approach includes regular inspections and treatments to keep your home and family safe. We also specialize in lawn and ornamental pest control to maintain the pristine appearance of Wesley Chapel’s beautiful neighborhoods.
serviceAreas.localContext: Wesley Chapel’s newer homes still face pest challenges due to Florida’s humid climate and rapid development.

serviceAreas.content: Horizon Pest Control offers top-tier pest management services in Tampa, FL, where dense urban living and humid weather create ideal conditions for pests. From downtown high-rises to suburban neighborhoods, we address common issues like roaches, rodents, and mosquitoes with precision. Our locally trained technicians understand Tampa’s unique pest pressures and provide tailored solutions to keep your home or business pest-free. We also offer commercial pest control for restaurants, offices, and other businesses.
